title: carex scopulus
intro: Carex scopulus, also known as the Rocky Mountain sedge, is a type of perennial grass-like plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. The medicinal properties of Carex scopulus lie in its roots, which contain a high concentration of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of using Carex scopulus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Carex scopulus often involve infusions or decoctions of the roots, which can be used as a tea or added to other herbal remedies for a range of health benefits.
